nodejs/exercise_mysql_limit1.htm.prompt: Complete the code to fetch records starting from a specific position:
nodejs/exercise_mysql_limit1.htm.code:
// Return 5 records starting from position 3 (the 4th record)
const sql = "SELECT * FROM customers LIMIT 5 @(6) 3";
nodejs/exercise_mysql_limit1.htm.solution:
// Return 5 records starting from position 3 (the 4th record)
const sql = "SELECT * FROM customers LIMIT 5 OFFSET 3";

html/exercise_html_forms1.htm.prompt: In the form below, add an input field with the type "button" and the value "OK".

html/exercise_html_forms2.htm.prompt: In the form below, add two radio buttons, both with the name "fav_language".
html/exercise_html_forms2.htm.solution:
<form>
<input type="radio" name="fav_language" value="html"> HTML
<input type="radio" name="fav_language" value="css"> CSS
</form>

html/exercise_html_form_elements1.htm.prompt: In the form below, add an empty drop down list with the name "cars".

html/exercise_html_form_elements2.htm.prompt: In the form below, add two option elements to the drop down list.

html/exercise_html_form_elements3.htm.prompt: In the form below, add a text area with the name "note".

html/exercise_html_form_input_types1.htm.prompt: In the form below, add an input field for text, with the name "username" .

html/exercise_html_form_input_types2.htm.prompt: In the form below, add a submit button with the value "Submit Form".
html/exercise_html_form_input_types2.htm.solution:
<form action="/action_page.php">
<input type="text" name="username">
<input type="submit" value="Submit Form">
</form>

html/exercise_html_form_input_types3.htm.prompt: Add two radio buttons with the name "color".
html/exercise_html_form_input_types3.htm.solution:
<form action="/action_page.php">
  Favorite color:
  Blue
  <input value="blue" type="radio" name="color">
  Red
  <input value="red" type="radio" name="color">
  <input type="submit">
</form>

html/exercise_html_form_input_types4.htm.prompt: In the form below, add an input field for button with the value "Click Me".

html/exercise_html_form_input_types5.htm.prompt: In the form below, add an input field that can only contain numbers, and use the correct input attributes to only allow numbers between 1 and 5.

html/exercise_html_forms_attributes1.htm.prompt: Add a submit button, and specify that the form should go to "/action_page.php".

html/exercise_html_forms_attributes2.htm.prompt: Specify that the form is submitted using the "POST" method.
html/exercise_html_forms_attributes2.htm.solution:
<form action="/action_page.php" method="post">
Name: <input type="text" name="name">
<input type="submit">
</form>
